vectors.name: Plain literal with URIs
vectors.purpose: Tests generation of a triple using full URIs and a plain literal.

vectors.name: Plain literal with CURIE from default context
vectors.purpose: Tests generation of a triple using a CURIE defined in the default context.

vectors.name: Default subject is BNode
vectors.purpose: Tests that a BNode is created if no explicit subject is set.

vectors.name: Literal with language tag
vectors.purpose: Tests that a plain literal is created with a language tag.
vectors.expected_nquads: _:b0 <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label> "A plain literal with a lang tag."@en-us .

vectors.name: Extended character set literal
vectors.purpose: Tests that a literal may be created using extended characters.

vectors.name: Typed literal
vectors.purpose: Tests creation of a literal with a datatype.

vectors.name: Tests 'a' generates rdf:type and object is implicit IRI
vectors.purpose: Verify that 'a' is an alias for rdf:type, and the object is created as an IRI.

vectors.name: Test prefix defined in @context
vectors.purpose: Generate an IRI using a prefix defined within an @context.

vectors.name: Test using an empty suffix
vectors.purpose: An empty suffix may be used.

vectors.name: Test object processing defines object
vectors.purpose: A property referencing an associative array gets object from subject of array.

vectors.name: Test object processing defines object with implicit BNode
vectors.purpose: If no @ is specified, a BNode is created, and will be used as the object of an enclosing property.

vectors.name: Multiple Objects for a Single Property
vectors.purpose: Tests that Multiple Objects are for a Single Property using array syntax.

vectors.name: Creation of an empty list
vectors.purpose: Tests that @list: [] generates an empty list.

vectors.name: Creation of a list with single element
vectors.purpose: Tests that @list generates a list.

vectors.name: Creation of a list with multiple elements
vectors.purpose: Tests that list with multiple elements.

vectors.name: Test type coercion to anyURI
vectors.purpose: Tests coercion of object to anyURI when specified.

vectors.name: Test type coercion to typed literal
vectors.purpose: Tests coercion of object to a typed literal when specified.

vectors.name: Test coercion of double value
vectors.purpose: Tests that a decimal value generates a xsd:double typed literal;.

vectors.name: Test coercion of integer value
vectors.purpose: Tests that a decimal value generates a xsd:integer typed literal.

vectors.name: Test coercion of boolean value
vectors.purpose: Tests that a decimal value generates a xsd:boolean typed literal.

vectors.name: Test list coercion with single element
vectors.purpose: Tests that an array with a single element on a property with @list coercion creates an RDF Collection.

vectors.name: Test creation of multiple types
vectors.purpose: Tests that @type with an array of types creates multiple types.

vectors.name: Simple named graph (Wikidata)
vectors.purpose: Using @graph with other keys places triples in a named graph.

vectors.name: Simple named graph
vectors.purpose: Signing a graph.

vectors.name: named graph with embedded named graph
vectors.purpose: Tests that named graphs containing named graphs flatten to single level of graph naming.

vectors.name: top-level graph with string subject reference
vectors.purpose: Tests graphs containing subject references as strings.

vectors.name: Reverse property
vectors.purpose: Tests conversion of reverse properties.

vectors.name: @context reordering
vectors.purpose: Tests that generated triples do not depend on order of @context.

vectors.name: @id reordering
vectors.purpose: Tests that generated triples do not depend on order of @id.

vectors.name: context properties reordering
vectors.purpose: Tests that generated triples do not depend on order of properties inside @context.

vectors.name: non-fractional numbers converted to xsd:double
vectors.purpose: xsd:double's canonical lexical is used when converting numbers without fraction that are coerced to xsd:double

vectors.name: Use nodeMapGeneration bnode labels
vectors.purpose: The toRDF algorithm does not relabel blank nodes; it reuses the counter from the nodeMapGeneration to generate new ones

vectors.name: Dataset with a IRI named graph
vectors.purpose: Basic use of creating a named graph using an IRI name

vectors.name: Dataset with a IRI named graph
vectors.purpose: Basic use of creating a named graph using a BNode name

vectors.name: Dataset with a default and two named graphs
vectors.purpose: Dataset with a default and two named graphs (IRI and BNode)

vectors.name: Dataset from node with embedded named graph
vectors.purpose: Embedding @graph in a node creates a named graph

vectors.name: Dataset from node with embedded named graph (bnode)
vectors.purpose: Embedding @graph in a node creates a named graph. Graph name is created if there is no subject

vectors.name: Blank nodes with reverse properties
vectors.purpose: Proper (re-)labeling of blank nodes if used with reverse properties.

vectors.name: adding new term
vectors.purpose: Expansion using a scoped context uses term scope for selecting proper term

vectors.purpose: Expansion using a scoped context uses term scope for selecting proper term

vectors.name: deep @context affects nested nodes
vectors.purpose: Expansion using a scoped context uses term scope for selecting proper term

vectors.name: scoped context layers on intemediate contexts
vectors.purpose: Expansion using a scoped context uses term scope for selecting proper term
vectors.expected_nquads:
_:b0 <http://example/a> _:b1 .
_:b0 <http://example/c> "C in example" .
_:b1 <http://example.com/c> "C in example.com" .
_:b1 <http://example/b> _:b2 .
_:b2 <http://example.com/a> "A in example.com" .
_:b2 <http://example.org/c> "C in example.org" .

vectors.name: Term scoping with embedded contexts.
vectors.purpose: Terms should make use of @vocab relative to the scope in which the term was defined.

vectors.name: Expansion with empty property-scoped context.
vectors.purpose: Adding a minimal/empty property-scoped context should not affect expansion of terms defined in the outer scope.

vectors.purpose: Term selection with language maps and @direction.
